ordering_note: Rules evaluate in LOAD ORDER (= file order here; both appended with -a), first match wins (f3 §2.3; catalog #28/#33). The never rule is listed FIRST, so it wins for uid=0 and suppresses root execve before the always rule sees it. Position matters: the inverse (never AFTER always, catalog #33) would NOT suppress.

rules.note: never action => SUPPRESSIVE. Contributes 0 additive volume; it REDUCES the always rule's net by removing root (uid=0) execve. A naive impl that counts this as additive HIGH execve volume overstates cost. Positioned FIRST so it actually suppresses (f3 §3.5).

unrestricted execve additive HIGH, BUT its net is reduced by the preceding never rule (root execve removed). The band below reflects the net after suppression of the uid=0 slice (modeled as the unrestricted band; the suppression removes daemon-root noise but the rate is workload-dependent, f3 §5).

dominant_tier: HIGH (additive), with a NEGATIVE/suppressive rule reducing it
rate_band_basis: f3 §6: always execve low5k/typ50k/high500k; never rule is 0 (suppressive), reducing the always net for uid=0 traffic
